variants.label: fallback_80
variants.note: fallback to incumbent on repeat>=5 or repeat>=4 with len<=80

variants.label: fallback_140
variants.note: fallback to incumbent on repeat>=5 or repeat>=4 with len<=140

variants.label: fallback_180
variants.note: fallback to incumbent on repeat>=5 or repeat>=4 with len<=180

variants.label: fallback_180_plus_trim
variants.note: same as fallback_180, then trim long internal repeats

top_repeated_predictions.text: I have not refused to go!" Ali-ahum answered the attorney Ababa; Ali-ahum said: "I submit to the tablet of the City and the attorney of my lord. Yesterday you questioned me and I answered you as foll
To Ennam-Aššur, sealed by Ali-ahum and <gap>-Ištar.

Investors of Šalim-Aššur may make demands Do this and dispatch the 2 tablets with verdicts of the City plus a copy of the testament, and hire an attorney and send Aluwa and the attorney as soon as possible
It is important that you set out and leave the very day you hear my letter, before word <gap> It is important that you set out and leave the very day you hear my letter, before word <gap>

To copper and 3 shekels of silver for his small goods, 3 shekels of silver <gap> I gave to Aluwa. I paid 4.5 shekels of silver, the fees from the City when he returned. <gap> minas of sickles, 3 shekels for his expenses…
To Šalim-Aššur; sealed by Man-mahir.
The 14 minas <gap> 8.6666 shekels of silver is the price of tin, textiles and donkeys.
Seal of Mannum-balum-Aššur son of Ṣilli-Adad, seal of Šu-Illil son of Mannum-kī-Aššur, seal of Puzur-Aššur son of Ataya owes 22 shekels of good silver to Ali-ahum Reckoned from the week of Ilīd…
